content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 21, 1993

Gregory P. Einhorn
Chief Deputy County Counsel
County of Butte
25 County Center Drive
Oroville, CA  95965-3380

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-93-165

Dear Mr. Einhorn:

     You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you
concerning the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/
Because your letter states only a general question, we consider it
to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c).2/

     This will confirm that your letter dated April 20, 1993,
accurately summarizes the telephone advice provided to you.  Your
letter asked for clarification regarding the type of membership
dues payments that are required to be disclosed under Section
86116.5(a)(3).

     We advised you that dues payments made by Butte County to
organizations in which Butte County or a department of the County
holds a membership are required to be reported if the organization
makes or solicits one or more expenditures equal to 10 percent of its total expenditures,
or $15,000 or more, during any calendar quarter, to influence
legislative or administrative action.  In contrast, dues paid by
the County for an employee's membership in an organization are not
reportable.  Such payments are considered part of the employee's
routine fringe benefits and are not reportable under the Act's
provisions.  (Sections 18.15 and 18616; Lowell Advice Letter, No.
A-86-283.)

_________________________

1/     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/     Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

I-93-165
Page 2


Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if
you have additional questions concerning this matter.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Lynda Cassady
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question: Your letter asked for clarification regarding the type of membership dues payments that are required to be disclosed under Section 86116.5(a)(3).
sections.conclusion: We advised you that dues payments made by Butte County to organizations in which Butte County or a department of the County holds a membership are required to be reported if the organization makes expenditures equal to 10 percent of its total expenditures, or $15,000 or more, during any calendar quarter, to influence legislative or administrative action. In contrast, dues paid by the County for an employee's membership in an organization are not reportable. Such payments are considered part of the employee's routine fringe benefits and are not reportable under the Act's provisions.
sections.question_synthetic: What types of membership dues payments made by a county are required to be disclosed under the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically distinguishing between dues for the county's institutional membership versus dues paid for an employee's membership?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that dues for the county's direct membership in organizations are reportable if the organization meets the expenditure threshold ($15,000 or 10% of total quarterly expenditures) to influence legislative/administrative action, but dues paid for an employee's individual membership are not reportable as they constitute routine fringe benefits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice letter (not formal written advice) confirming prior telephone guidance. The document clearly identifies both the question and conclusion/advice provided by the FPPC.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your letter asked for clarification regarding the type of membership dues payments that are required to be disclosed under Section 86116.5(a)(3).

CONCLUSION: We advised you that dues payments made by Butte County to organizations in which Butte County or a department of the County holds a membership are required to be reported if the organization makes expenditures equal to 10 percent of its total expenditures, or $15,000 or more, during any calendar quarter, to influence legislative or administrative action. In contrast, dues paid by the County for an employee's membership in an organization are not reportable. Such payments are considered part of the employee's routine fringe benefits and are not reportable under the Act's provisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed informal telephone advice regarding which membership dues payments must be disclosed under the Political Reform Act's lobbying provisions, distinguishing between reportable county membership dues and non-reportable employee membership dues.
